title: 2026 Migration Scorecard Target >50% Before Q1 End
description: CLARA contributed to achieving 16+ migrations (>50% of the 32-migration 2026 target) before end of Q1. Azmain explicitly credited CLARA's organisational alignment effect in enabling this achievement.
before_state: Manual tracking via 300-slide PowerPoint decks, no real-time visibility into migration status
after_state: Structured data model, weekly Portfolio Review discipline, 16+ migrations completed
notes: Demonstrated during CLARA demo to Product & Tech teams on 13 March with ~40-50 attendees including MD Product & Technology (Cihan).

title: HD Model Adoption Metrics Now Tracked in CLARA
description: CLARA now provides real-time HD model adoption visibility: 21 customers using HD models in production out of 111 with HD models available. Provides quantified adoption blocker data that was previously scattered across spreadsheets.
before_state: No centralised HD model adoption metrics; scattered data quality issues
after_state: Structured tracking — 21 of 111 customers identified as active, blockers to adoption identified
notes: Cited by Azmain as important enough for Monday programme review focus.

title: ~4,000-5,000 Users Imported as CLARA Viewers via SSO
description: All users under Andy Frappe (President, Moody's Analytics) imported as CLARA viewers with SSO access. Enables enterprise-wide visibility into migration and adoption data. Only specific users have edit access (CSMs + leadership), but full read access unlocked.
before_state: Access restricted to ~15-20 core CSM team members
after_state: ~4,000-5,000 viewers with SSO access; anyone can log in to see everything
notes: Announced by Azmain in Product & Tech demo on 13 March.

title: MD Product & Technology Personally Endorses CLARA
description: Cihan (Managing Director, Product & Technology) convened entire product and engineering team (~40-50 attendees) for CLARA demo. Personally uses CLARA daily to understand blockers, customer status, and dissatisfaction. Encouraged team exploration and feedback.
before_state: Product & Technology team largely unaware of CLARA; perceived as CSM-specific tool
after_state: Full team awareness, active encouragement for adoption and feedback from MD level
notes: Strongest product/tech endorsement since Andy Frappe initial demo.

title: Cross-OU Demand Validated by Banking & Life Insurance
description: Banking leadership (Gina Greer, Olivier) and Life Insurance leadership (Jack Cheyne, Christian Curran) both engaged in knowledge-sharing sessions requested by Diya. Both teams at same stage insurance was in November 2025. Both recognised value and are considering collaboration.
before_state: Programme scope limited to insurance division; cross-OU interest unknown
after_state: Validated cross-OU demand from two additional divisions; Life SLT and Banking leadership actively exploring reuse
notes: Multiple leadership conversations documented across 9-10 March.

title: First External Team Member (Idris) Onboarded for TSR Automation
description: Idris formally onboarded as first non-core-team member approved for dedicated programme time (approved by Ben Brookes). Working on TSR automation as concrete project. Becomes AI ambassador back to risk advisory team.
before_state: Programme limited to core insurance CS team
after_state: Cross-team expansion model proved viable; first external team member producing value
notes: Explicitly approved by Ben Brookes.

title: ISLTR Cross-OU App Factory Showcase Planned
description: Wednesday showcase meeting timed for Insurance Senior Leadership Team in London. Agenda: CS team AI initiatives across OUs, Idrees's segment-agnostic retention dashboard, ROB AI app, App Factory infrastructure. First formal cross-OU senior leadership presentation.
before_state: Cross-OU interest ad-hoc; no formal multi-OU senior leadership forum
after_state: Structured cross-OU showcase scheduled with ISLTR members; portfolio-level visibility
notes: Scheduled by Richard and Idrees for week of 17 March.

title: AON Data Interest: First Sales-Side Tool Request
description: Natalie Bath (reports to Helen Ryder) saw MENA's agent demo at Miami sales connect and asked Idrees to run agent view on AON data. Richard offered Salesforce tickets and CLARA insights. First signal of sales-side interest in programme tools.
before_state: Programme tools viewed as CS-specific
after_state: Sales teams actively requesting tool capability after seeing demo
notes: Rob Fulber meeting Dan Dick (Global Head of Cat Risk at AON) in London.

title: AIG Identified as Strategic Client for Build in Five
description: Intelligence emerged that AIG has signed strategic deals with Anthropic and Palantir and is using MCP servers for underwriting. Elevates Build in Five from internal demo tool to strategic client engagement vehicle.
before_state: Build in Five framed as internal demo/sales enablement tool
after_state: Strategic client engagement vehicle with measurable business development opportunity
notes: Richard positioned two-tier strategy: 5-min dashboard for standard clients, MCP workflows for sophisticated clients like AIG.

title: Cross-OU Insurance ROI Framework Sharing Initiated
description: Richard committed to share insurance team's ROI approach and approval framework with Banking and Life teams. Creates repeatable process for cross-OU expansion and reduces re-work.
before_state: Each OU would need to build own business case from scratch
after_state: Repeatable ROI/approval framework available for deployment across OUs
notes: Committed in sessions with Gina Greer, Olivier, Jack Cheyne.

title: Build in Five: Custom Dashboard in 5 Minutes vs 18 Months
description: Martin's Build in Five dashboard builder demonstrates five-minute custom dashboard creation. Comparison: Golden Bear case required 18 months for Guidewire Policy Centre webhook, which Build in Five can now prototype in ~5 minutes.
before_state: Custom dashboard requests take 18 months; customers often abandon due to timeline
after_state: Prototype dashboard creation in 5 minutes with whiteboard-to-API-to-dashboard workflow
notes: Richard compared it to Databricks Genie. Ben Brookes focused on product positioning rather than features, indicating technical confidence.

title: Build in Five Dashboard Builder Reaches Commercial Maturity
description: Martin's dashboard builder exceeded all expectations for 12-week assignment. Delivered: drag-drop UI, white-labelling with custom branding, live Risk Modeller API connections, AI assistant mode, save/load configurations, preview mode.
before_state: Scope described as 'drag-and-drop with three options per component'
after_state: Near-complete white-label platform with full API integration and component generation
notes: Richard compared to Databricks Genie (commercial product). Martin's 12-week clock ticking but output justifies investment many times over.

title: Friday PM App Built by 30 Concurrent AI Agents in One Day
description: Azmain designed and built a complete project management application (Friday) using 30 concurrent AI agents in a single day. Features: Kanban boards, list views, timeline views, milestones, budgeting, decision tracking, stakeholder heat maps.
before_state: Manual slide-based Wednesday advisory project reviews with no structured workflow
after_state: Automated PM tool with multiple view options and bidirectional sync to CLARA for IRP projects
notes: Endorsed by Diana as replacement for manual advisory project reviews.

title: App Factory Pivots to MCP Server Architecture
description: BenVH pivoted entire App Factory core from UI-driven platform to MCP server architecture. Central middleware eliminates need for per-app LLM pipelines. Apps connect to single central MCP server, enabling inter-app workflows.
before_state: Separate bespoke LLM deployments per application; no inter-app communication
after_state: Unified MCP server handling AI worker lifecycle; any reusable process moves to central platform
notes: Formally announced and designed by BenVH with Richard and Azmain.

title: Gainsight Integration Governance Formalised
description: Kathryn Palkovics convened formal Gainsight integration meeting. Clear decisions: CLARA is IRP-specific, Gainsight is overall CSM BAU (complementary). One-way integration (read only, Cases and Case Feed phase 1). Kathryn Palkovics volunteered to design governance decision tree for App Factory intake.
before_state: Gainsight relationship unclear; integration approach uncoordinated; Kathryn Palkovics seen as detractor
after_state: Clear integration scope, appointed governance gatekeeper, enthusiastic enterprise stakeholder
notes: Attended by Tina Palumbo (exec strategy), Nadim (technical), Rajesh (data integration).

title: Executive Sponsor Endorses Three-Pillar Programme Structure
description: 23 Feb governance session with Diya produced strategic clarity: (1) IRP portfolio governance, (2) platform-embedded customer intelligence, (3) internal productivity and revenue acceleration. Confirmed 12-week resource plan and post-twelve-week business case requirement.
before_state: Unclear strategic direction; unclear resource commitment; programme sprawl across six workstreams
after_state: Clear three-pillar framework; confirmed resources (Martin 75%, Chris 50%, Nikhil 50%, BenVH 50%); defined success criteria
notes: Diya directed CLARA focus to insurance IRP with no Life roadmap.

title: Alembic Migration Heads Consolidated from 113 to One
description: Alembic migration heads consolidated from 113 to one base level. Cleared deployment synchronisation problems that had plagued the system. Pushed through dev to staging without breakage.
before_state: 113 migration heads accumulated, causing deployment sync issues
after_state: Single consolidated migration base; stable deployment pipeline
notes: Major infrastructure stabilisation milestone.

title: AWS Bedrock API Authentication Working via IAM Roles
description: Nikhil confirmed Bedrock API key working on local environment using OU-based IAM roles (no hard-coded keys, security-compliant). Unblocks LLM integration features in CLARA.
before_state: Personal Claude accounts used for proprietary Moody's work; security audit exposure
after_state: Corporate Bedrock API integrated; no hard-coded credentials; scalable across OUs
notes: Nikhil's IAM expertise solved a problem that had eluded the team for weeks.

title: CLARA Release Cycle Optimised: Two-Week Friday Releases
description: Azmain transitioned CLARA to two-week release cycle with Friday releases, replacing unsustainable weekly Wednesday cadence. Enables more thorough testing and CSM-friendly timing.
before_state: Weekly Wednesday release cadence unsustainable; no time for stabilisation
after_state: Two-week Friday releases; improved stability; clearer feature grouping
notes: Releases on Friday give team weekend to explore before Monday Portfolio Review.

title: CLARA Feedback Backlog Systematised
description: Chris discovered that many early feedback items were already fixed but never documented. Implemented consolidated feedback triage process. Early Feb items were already resolved — systematic gap made team appear unresponsive when product was improving.
before_state: Feedback not tracked; fixes not connected to reported issues; team appeared unresponsive
after_state: Systematic feedback triage; documented resolution; transparency to stakeholders
notes: Chris worked through the list and discovered the resolution pattern.

title: Migration Dashboard Redesigned for Scorecard Alignment
description: Migration dashboard completely redesigned to align with Diya's scorecard needs: Hosting Plus consolidated under Risk Link, RBO under Risk Browser (matching scorecard language), new Scorecard tab as default, quarterly view added.
before_state: Confusing grey box with inaccurate priority counter; inconsistent terminology; no quarterly view
after_state: Clean, scorecard-aligned dashboard; quarterly visibility; terminology matches executive requirements
notes: Detailed design session with Stacy and Natalia.

title: CLARA Dev Team Expanded: Chris and Nikhil Onboarded
description: Chris onboarded primarily for CLARA bug fixes and feedback triage. Nikhil split 50% CLARA / 50% App Factory. Frees Azmain from sole developer role and creates capacity for strategic work.
before_state: Azmain sole developer consuming 100% time on CLARA; zero progress on other workstreams
after_state: Distributed team; Azmain free for programme management; bug backlog addressable
notes: Chris already delivering value. Nikhil's IAM expertise solved Bedrock API problem.

title: Two Rotating Graduates Approved for April 7 Start
description: Two rotating graduates (one New York, one London) approved to begin 7 April. Will take over bug fix backlog from Chris. Provides sustainable maintenance capacity and knowledge distribution.
before_state: All maintenance and bug fixes dependent on Azmain + Chris
after_state: Dedicated grad resources for stabilisation; knowledge distributed
notes: Approved in Diya governance session.

title: Full CLARA Knowledge Base Generated for AI Assistant
description: Azmain generated full user-facing documentation and knowledge base for CLARA. In dev, pending promotion to production. Intended to power future AI assistant chatbot within CLARA.
before_state: Zero formal documentation; outdated READMEs; Azmain was only person who could answer questions
after_state: Complete knowledge base; ready for chatbot integration; documentation living in system
notes: Generated at 2am — demonstrates Azmain's commitment but also bandwidth pressure.

title: Salesforce CDR Database Access Unblocked
description: Dan Flemington brought Julia Valencia (sales analytics) as contact with direct Salesforce CDR database access. This access had been a blocker for months. Opens pathway for programmatic Salesforce integration.
before_state: Salesforce data access blocked; no direct contact with data owners
after_state: Direct access to CDR database through trusted stakeholder (Julia Valencia)
notes: Julia confirmed as new stakeholder with direct CDR access.

title: Salesforce Integration Strategy: One Build, Four Consumers
description: Clear Salesforce integration strategy: one-way read-only, Cases and Case Feed phase 1. App Factory MCP server as middleware, built once and consumed by all apps. Four consumer groups: Bernard (sentiment), Courtney (HD blockers), Kevin (tickets), CLARA (blocker intelligence).
before_state: Multiple teams building independent Salesforce integrations; no coordination; duplicate effort
after_state: Single integration serving four consumer teams via App Factory middleware
notes: Formal design session with clear decisions.
